mechanism: If the Fed cannot reliably estimate r*, it cannot calibrate the appropriate policy rate, contributing to effective Taylor coefficient compression. The 2.4-3.4pp gap between competing r* estimates (HLW vs fiscal-sustainability) creates structural policy uncertainty that constrains aggressive rate-setting.
regime_dependence: Worsens as fiscal dominance increases the wedge between model-implied and sustainability-consistent neutral rates. Fed SEP dispersion widening is real-time evidence of this uncertainty.
